page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369HB processor clocked at 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network interfaces with a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.hfc7.12xlarge demonstrates balanced compute and memory capabilities. It achieves a PassMark CPU score of 40,369.24 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,926.0, both falling within the average tier. Notably, the instance exhibits strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression, reaching 206.84 GB/sec, which places it in the top 25% of tested servers. Memory performance is also strong, with a PassMark memory score of 2,865.83 and a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 2,779.33 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the CPU supports AVX-512 vector extensions, delivering 68.85 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B parameter LLM.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.hfc7.12xlarge is its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local NVMe storage, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or high-I/O local caching workloads. However, its dedicated core allocation and high base clock speed of 3.3 GHz provide predictable, non-shared compute performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers high compute density for parallelizable workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale CPU-based inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor clocked at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and memory operations, alongside average performance in standard CPU and cryptographic tests. Due to its dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed, the instance is highly efficient for compute-dense, parallel workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, though it is constrained by the lack of local storage and a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-threaded decompression performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and a 3.3 GHz clock speed.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core compute-optimized platform featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors for parallel processing and multi-threaded workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and memory benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel compute tasks
